text: A userdata object representing an area trigger in the game.
Returns the id of an area trigger.
Returns the world id of the area trigger. Keep in mind that area triggers can move between worlds, so world id might change!
return: The _current_ world id of the area trigger.

text: Bind a callback that checks if the area trigger can be erased.
params: The name of the Lua function to bind.
The object that will receive the callback. (optional)

text: Bind the lua function to be used when checking for player interaction with the area trigger
params: The name of the Lua function to bind.
The object that will receive the callback. (optional)

text: Bind the lua function to be called when the area trigger is destroyed
params: The name of the Lua function to bind.
The object that will receive the callback. (optional)

text: Binds an area trigger's onEnter event to a custom callback. The onEnter event is triggered when an object enters the trigger area.
The callback receives:
@list
self (table) – The class instance.
trigger ([AreaTrigger]) – The area trigger instance.
results (table) – A table of [Character, characters] and/or [Body, bodies] and/or [Harvestable, harvestables] and/or [Lift, lifts] and/or [AreaTrigger, areaTriggers].
@list
@code
function MyClass.onEnter( self, trigger, results ) ...
@code
params: The name of the Lua function to bind.
The object that will receive the callback. (optional)

text: Bind the lua function to be used when an interactable area trigger is erased.
params: The name of the Lua function to bind.
The object that will receive the callback. (optional)

text: Binds an area trigger's onExit event to a custom callback. The onExit event is triggered when an object leaves the trigger area.
The callback receives:
@list
self (table) – The class instance.
trigger ([AreaTrigger]) – The area trigger instance.
results (table) – A table of [Character, characters] and/or [Body, bodies] and/or [Harvestable, harvestables] and/or [Lift, lifts] and/or [AreaTrigger, areaTriggers].
@list
@code
function MyClass.onExit( self, trigger, results ) ...
@code
params: The name of the Lua function to bind.
The object that will receive the callback. (optional)

text: Bind the lua function to be used when an area trigger is destroyed.
params: The name of the Lua function to bind.
The object that will receive the callback. ( optional )

text: Bind the lua function to be used when an interactable area trigger's host object is unloaded.
Note: Only the game host can know if a host object is unloaded, to a connected client it will look like the host object was destroyed.
params: The name of the Lua function to bind.
The object that will receive the callback. ( optional )

text: Bind the lua function to be used when a player interacts with an area trigger
params: The name of the Lua function to bind.
The object that will receive the callback. (optional)

text: Bind the lua function to be used when the area trigger is hit by a melee attack.
Requires that areaTriggerProxyType has been set to [sm.areaTrigger.areaTriggerProxyType, sm.areaTrigger.areaTriggerProxyType.melee]
params: The name of the Lua function to bind.
The object that will receive the callback. (optional)

text: Binds an area trigger's onProjectile event to a custom callback. The onProjectile event is triggered if a projectile collides with the trigger area
params: The name of the Lua function to bind.
The object that will receive the callback. (optional)

text: Binds an area trigger's onStay event to a custom callback. The onStay event is triggered every tick as long as an object is staying inside of the trigger area.
The callback receives:
@list
self (table) – The class instance.
trigger ([AreaTrigger]) – The area trigger instance.
results (table) – A table of [Character, characters] and/or [Body, bodies] and/or [Harvestable, harvestables] and/or [Lift, lifts] and/or [AreaTrigger, areaTriggers].
@list
@code
function MyClass.onStay( self, trigger, results ) ...
@code
params: The name of the Lua function to bind.
The object that will receive the callback. (optional)

text: Destroys an area trigger.
params: The area trigger to be destroyed.

text: Gets the trigger collisions for characters inside the area trigger
return: A table of character trigger collisions.

text: Gets the contents of the area trigger.
Returns a table of [Character, characters] and/or [Body, bodies] and/or [Harvestable, harvestables] and/or [Lift, lifts] and/or [AreaTrigger, areaTriggers].
Gets the trigger collisions for harvestables inside the area trigger
return: A table of harvestable trigger collisions.

text: Returns the attached host [sm.interactable, interactable].
Returns the id of an area trigger.
Gets the trigger collisions for shapes inside the area trigger
Will only return one [Shape] for each [Body] that is colliding with the [AreaTrigger].
return: A table of shape trigger collisions.

text: Returns the size of an area trigger.
Returns the user data set on the area trigger.
return: The user data set on this trigger

text: Returns the world id of the area trigger. Keep in mind that area triggers can move between worlds, so world id might change!
return: The _current_ world id of the area trigger.

text: Returns the world max corner position of an area trigger.
return: The area trigger's max corner position.

text: Returns the world min corner position of an area trigger.
return: The area trigger's min corner position.

text: Returns the world position of an area trigger.
Returns the world rotation of an area trigger.
Returns true if the [AreaTrigger] is in contact with destructable terrain.
When set to true the area trigger can calculate which characters are inside of the trigger and get the collision information.
with a call to [AreaTrigger]: getCharacters
Sets whether the areatrigger should be destroyed immediately upon being erased or simply invoke the bound OnErase callback. True by default.
Sets the time it takes to erase this trigger if it is erasable.
params: The time it takes to erase this area trigger.

text: When set to true the area trigger can calculate which harvestables are inside of the trigger and get the collision information.
with a call to [AreaTrigger]: getHarvestables
When set to true the area trigger will include individual shapes in its content list.
params: Include shapes in content on or off.

text: Shape detection is off by default. When set to true the area trigger can calculate which shapes are inside of the trigger
with a call to [AreaTrigger]: getShapes
Sets the new size of an area trigger.
Sets whether VoxelTerrain can destroy this area trigger by voxel addition.
Sets the liquid type of the area trigger. (See [sm.areaTrigger.liquidType])
Only has an effect on area triggers with the 'water' proxy type; setting it on any other proxy type has no effect.
Currently only 'lava' has special behavior: it occasionally destroys submerged shapes. Lava behavior requires
shapes to be included in the content list via [AreaTrigger]: setIncludeShapesInContent.
Sets the new world position of an area trigger.
params: The area trigger's new world position.

text: Sets the new world rotation of an area trigger.
params: The area trigger's new world rotation.

meta: Checks if two instances of [AreaTrigger] refer to the same AreaTrigger.
